@graph.title: "Sagebrush rebellion" is just another name for a public lands heist
@graph.creator: Charlie Callison

text.@value: No unreserved unappropriated public lands conveyed to any state pursuant to this act shall be conveyed, transferred, or otherwise disposed of except pursuant to the specific authority of a state law enacted on or after the date on which such state acquired such lands pursuant to this act.

html.@value: No unreserved unappropriated public lands conveyed to any state pursuant to this act shall be conveyed, transferred, or otherwise disposed of except pursuant to the specific authority of a state law enacted on or after the date on which such state acquired such lands pursuant to this act.

text.@value: Hatch: “My bill is designed to return control of our destiny to the people of Utah by transferring title to the unappropriated public lands to the state capital and, from there, to the county authorities and, ultimately, to private citizens.” (12)

html.@value: Hatch: “My bill is designed to return control of our destiny to the people of Utah by transferring title to the unappropriated public lands to the state capital and, from there, to the county authorities and, ultimately, to private citizens.” (12)

text.@value: DeVoto reporting on mcCarran that he “succeeded in getting the Grazing Service merged with the General Land Office to form the Bureau of Land Management. Then he got the new agency’s appropriations reduced to the point where it was administratively impotent, a captive of the stockmen it was suppose to regulate.”

html.@value: DeVoto reporting on mcCarran that he “succeeded in getting the Grazing Service merged with the General Land Office to form the Bureau of Land Management. Then he got the new agency’s appropriations reduced to the point where it was administratively impotent, a captive of the stockmen it was suppose to regulate.”

text.@value: Callison calls the Newsweek Angry West story filled with “so many factual errors and selective quotes that it amounted to a travesty of the profession of journalism.” (12)

text.@value: California attempted to pass its own sagebrush legislation but Jerry Brown vetoed the bill on Sept 20 1979.
